commands.review_summary:
This PR adds a reserved meta-string guard for `current`, `self`, `this`, and `me` inside the session delivery target resolver.

PR surface: Source +10. Total +10 across 1 file.

Reproducibility: yes. for the source-level path: current `main` accepts `explicitTo` as a raw string, and `resolveAgentDeliveryPlan` can continue carrying the original value after resolver processing. I did not run a live Telegram reproduction in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Contributor or maintainer follow-up is needed because the patch has a blocking delivery-path defect and lacks real behavior proof; automation should not repair an external PR while the proof gate is unmet.

target.title: #91372: reject reserved meta-strings ("current", "self", etc.) as literal delivery targets
target.body:
## Summary

Reject reserved meta-strings like "current", "self", "this", and "me" as literal delivery targets in the gateway session-target resolver, preventing outbound messages from being routed to unintended recipients.

## Root Cause

`resolveSessionDeliveryTarget` accepts any string as `explicitTo` without validating whether it is a reserved meta-string. When sub-agent or plugin code passes `"current"` (intending to mean "the current session"), the resolver falls through to a literal channel lookup. If a public channel happens to be named `@current`, the message is delivered there — a production hard-to-debug leak.

## Real behavior proof

**Environment**: local gateway with `resolveSessionDeliveryTarget` unit-test paths.

**Change**: 11-line guard clause in `targets-session.ts` — adds a `Set` of reserved meta-strings and rejects them before they reach the channel-specific resolver.

**Evidence (before)**: `target="current"` → `resolveExplicitDeliveryTargetCompat({ rawTarget: "current" })` → channel lookup → wrong recipient (public @current channel on Telegram).

**Evidence (after)**: `target="current"` → `RESERVED_TARGET_META_STRINGS.has("current")` → `rawExplicitTo = undefined` → returns session-level `lastTo` or `undefined`, never a channel-directory lookup.

**Not tested**: Full integration test with a real Telegram bot (requires live credentials).

## Regression Test Plan

- [x] Existing `target-session.test.ts` suite continues to pass (no functional behavior change for normal `@user`, `#channel`, `+123456` targets)
- [ ] `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/auto-reply/reply/get-reply-directives.target-session.test.ts`

---

*AI-assisted: built with Claude Code*
